name: Shreyash Londhe

projects.name: AI Portfolio Assistant
projects.description: Production-ready full-stack portfolio with a resume-aware AI chatbot powered by FastAPI and GroqCloud. The backend injects structured resume context into every LLM prompt server-side, keeping API keys and personal data off the client. SQLite persists chat messages across sessions.
features: Resume-aware AI chat — GroqCloud LLM answers recruiter questions using structured server-side context injection
FastAPI backend with SQLite message persistence and CORS-safe REST endpoints
Floating chat widget with typing indicators and Framer Motion transitions
API keys and resume data never exposed to the client
Backend deployed on Render; full-stack production architecture

projects.name: Hakimi Establishment Website
projects.description: Performance-optimised React e-commerce frontend with three-tier lazy loading — React.lazy code splitting, Intersection Observer-driven API calls via a custom useIntersectionTrigger hook, and LazyImage media lazy loading. Initial load time reduced by 66% (1.5s to 0.5s) and initial data transfer reduced by 67% (150 KB to 50 KB).
features: Three-tier lazy loading: code splitting (React.lazy) + API lazy loading (useIntersectionTrigger) + media lazy loading (LazyImage)
Custom useIntersectionTrigger hook defers products API call until scroll — 66% faster initial load
ProductSkeleton with CSS pulse animation shows 5 placeholder cards during fetch — zero layout shift
Two-flag state pattern (loadingProducts / productsLoaded) prevents re-fetches and skeleton flicker
Zustand global cart state; useAdminSocket WebSocket hook for live admin order updates
Fully responsive grid: 2-column mobile, 3-column tablet, 5-column desktop

projects.name: AuraStream
projects.description: Production-grade Netflix-inspired MERN streaming platform. Supports up to 5 independent profiles per user account, each with isolated search history, continue-watching progress, and Cloudinary avatar images. A single JWT cookie carries both userId and profileId, scoping all data automatically. Content is fetched from the TMDB API through a shared service layer. Built on a fully modular Express 5 backend.
features: Multi-profile system: up to 5 profiles per account, each fully isolated by profileId in every DB query
Single JWT cookie carries both userId and profileId — /switchprofile endpoint regenerates token instantly
TMDB integration: trending, trailers, details, similar titles, and categories via a shared fetchFromTMDB service
Per-profile continue-watching: currentTime, season, episode, and totalDuration persisted in MongoDB
Cloudinary image pipeline: Multer memory-buffers upload → base64 data URI → streams to Cloudinary (zero disk I/O)
Modular Express 5 architecture: routes → controllers → models → middleware, no business logic in route files
Per-profile search history with atomic $push / $pull and indexed profileId for fast filtering
bcryptjs hashing (salt rounds = 10), HTTP-only SameSite-strict secure-in-prod JWT cookies

projects.name: Osiris Level Editor
projects.description: Feature-complete isometric tile-based level editor built in C++ with SDL3. Implements a full bidirectional isometric coordinate projection system, a 6-layer scene graph with per-layer visibility and alpha blending, per-tile scale storage, a pixel-accurate collider editor, and a CSV save/load pipeline that outputs data directly consumable by a game engine.
features: Bidirectional isometric projection: cartesian ↔ screen coordinate conversion with zoom (0.5x–3x) and right-click pan
6-layer system (Terrain, Player, Furniture, Enemy, NPCs, Portal) with per-layer visibility toggling and alpha compositing
Per-tile scale factor stored per cell; global brush scale adjusted live with [ / ] hotkeys
Pixel-accurate collider editor: click-to-set per-edge offsets (T/B/L/R) with red bounding box overlay
CSV pipeline: tile IDs, scale values, collider data, and level metadata all round-trip through flat CSV files
Dynamic tile loader: scans asset directory at startup, tolerates up to 20 consecutive missing tile IDs
Smooth tile-snap camera: Space key lerps viewport to nearest isometric tile center via SNAP_SPEED interpolation
Multi-level workflow: Ctrl+N/D to add/delete levels, arrow keys to switch, Ctrl+S saves all levels

strengths: Strong problem solving and algorithmic thinking
Clean backend architecture design with separation of concerns
Performance engineering — measurable optimisation with real metrics
Comfortable working in team codebases (collaborative C++ project)
